issue.title: [FEATURE] Revise dump zone thresholds for 1M models and add planning/zone readiness indicators

affected_files.role: zone logic, MI formula, threshold constants

analysis.summary: This feature added a five-state context zone indicator system (P/C/D/X/Z) layered on top of the existing MI formula. The implementation uses absolute token thresholds for 1M models and utilization ratios for standard models, synchronized across all three implementations.
analysis.details:
The zone system addresses the problem that a single 40% context utilization threshold was inaccurate for 1M context models. The graduated approach (P→C→D→X→Z) replaces the binary dump zone concept with five states that communicate session health at a glance.

1M models use absolute token thresholds: P < 70k, C 70k–100k, D 100k–250k, X 250k–275k, Z >= 275k. Standard models use utilization ratios with a 30k warning buffer before the dump zone.

The implementation added ZoneInfo dataclass, get_context_zone() function, and 10 zone threshold constants to the package. The standalone Python and Node.js implementations were updated in sync. The MI formula itself was untouched — zones are an additive overlay.

One deviation from the original spec: the issue proposed X at exactly 250k and Z > 250k, but the implementation uses X as a range (250k–275k) with Z >= 275k, providing a 25k buffer.

options.name: Already implemented (PR #21)
options.summary: Five-state zone indicators with model-size-aware thresholds, synchronized across all implementations

files_to_modify.changes: Added get_context_zone(), zone ANSI colors, zone display
Added getContextZone(), zone ANSI colors, zone display

pros: Thorough test coverage (40 tests across Python + Node.js)
cons: Minor spec deviation: X zone is a range (250k–275k) rather than a point (250k)
options.risk_details: Additive feature — existing MI formula and behavior unchanged
